ground.cypher:
MATCH
    p1 = (a:Article {articleId: 1})-[r1:FROM_DEPARTMENT]->(b:Department {departmentNo: 101}),
    p2 = (a)-[r2:VARIANT_OF]->(c:Product {productCode: 2001}),
    p3 = (c)-[r3:PURCHASED]->(d:Article {articleId: 102})
RETURN p1, p2, p3
ground.question: How does the department handling article 1 influence the product variants and subsequent purchases of similar articles like 102, considering their departmental origin and sales history?

cypher: MATCH (a1:Article {articleId: 1}) -[:FROM_DEPARTMENT]->(d:Department),       (a2:Article {articleId: 102}) -[:FROM_DEPARTMENT]->(d),       (a1)-[:VARIANT_OF]->(p:Product),       (a2)-[:VARIANT_OF]->(p),       (a1)-[:CUSTOMERS_ALSO_LIKE]->(a3:Article),       (a2)-[:CUSTOMERS_ALSO_LIKE]->(a3)\nRETURN d, p, COLLECT(a3) AS similarArticles
